channel.title: Daily AI Digest

item.title: VLAA-GUI: Knowing When to Stop, Recover, and Search, A Modular Framework for GUI Automation

item.title: StyleID: A Perception-Aware Dataset and Metric for Stylization-Agnostic Facial Identity Recognition
item.description: 📄 Featured on Hugging Face Daily Papers — Creative face stylization aims to render portraits in diverse visual idioms such as cartoons, sketches, and paintings while retaining recognizable identity. However, current identity encoders, which are typically trained and calibrated on natural photographs, exhibit severe brittleness under styl...

item.title: WorldMark: A Unified Benchmark Suite for Interactive Video World Models
item.description: 📄 Featured on Hugging Face Daily Papers — Interactive video generation models such as Genie, YUME, HY-World, and Matrix-Game are advancing rapidly, yet every model is evaluated on its own benchmark with private scenes and trajectories, making fair cross-model comparison impossible. Existing public benchmarks offer useful metrics such as ...

item.title: UniGenDet: A Unified Generative-Discriminative Framework for Co-Evolutionary Image Generation and Generated Image Detection
item.description: 📄 New in cs.CV — In recent years, significant progress has been made in both image generation and generated image detection. Despite their rapid, yet largely independent, development, these two fields have evolved distinct architectural paradigms: the former predominantly relies on generative networks, while the ...

item.title: Seeing Fast and Slow: Learning the Flow of Time in Videos
item.description: 📄 New in cs.CV, cs.AI — How can we tell whether a video has been sped up or slowed down? How can we generate videos at different speeds? Although videos have been central to modern computer vision research, little attention has been paid to perceiving and controlling the passage of time. In this paper, we study time as ...

item.title: Context Unrolling in Omni Models
item.description: 📄 New in cs.CV — We present Omni, a unified multimodal model natively trained on diverse modalities, including text, images, videos, 3D geometry, and hidden representations. We find that such training enables Context Unrolling, where the model explicitly reasons across multiple modal representations before produc...

item.title: TingIS: Real-time Risk Event Discovery from Noisy Customer Incidents at Enterprise Scale
item.description: 📄 New in cs.CL, cs.AI — Real-time detection and mitigation of technical anomalies are critical for large-scale cloud-native services, where even minutes of downtime can result in massive financial losses and diminished user trust. While customer incidents serve as a vital signal for discovering risks missed by monitorin...

item.title: Temporal Taskification in Streaming Continual Learning: A Source of Evaluation Instability
item.description: 📄 New in cs.LG — Streaming Continual Learning (CL) typically converts a continuous stream into a sequence of discrete tasks through temporal partitioning. We argue that this temporal taskification step is not a neutral preprocessing choice, but a structural component of evaluation: different valid splits of the s...

item.title: Evaluation of Automatic Speech Recognition Using Generative Large Language Models
item.description: 📄 New in cs.CL — Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR) is traditionally evaluated using Word Error Rate (WER), a metric that is insensitive to meaning. Embedding-based semantic metrics are better correlated with human perception, but decoder-based Large Language Models (LLMs) remain underexplored for this task. This...

item.title: Fine-Tuning Regimes Define Distinct Continual Learning Problems
item.description: 📄 New in cs.LG — Continual learning (CL) studies how models acquire tasks sequentially while retaining previously learned knowledge. Despite substantial progress in benchmarking CL methods, comparative evaluations typically keep the fine-tuning regime fixed. In this paper, we argue that the fine-tuning regime, de...

item.title: BerriAI / litellm
item.description: 💬 Major HN discussion (500 comments) · 🔥 Trending on HN (938 pts) — Python SDK, Proxy Server (AI Gateway) to call 100+ LLM APIs in OpenAI (or native) format, with cost tracking, guardrails, loadbalancing and logging. [Bedrock, Azure, OpenAI, VertexAI, Cohere, Anthropic, Sagemaker, HuggingFace, VLLM, NVIDIA NIM]

item.title: onyx-dot-app / onyx
item.description: 💬 Major HN discussion (160 comments) · ⭐ 213 GitHub stars today — Open Source AI Platform - AI Chat with advanced features that works with every LLM

item.title: From Chatbots to Autonomous Analysts: A Deep Dive into Google’s Gemini Deep Research Agent
item.description: 🆕 New article — <div class="medium-feed-item"><p class="medium-feed-image"><a href="https://curateai.medium.com/from-chatbots-to-autonomous-analysts-a-deep-dive-into-googles-gemini-deep-research-agent-ddf377f1b2fd?source=rss------llm-5"><img src="https://cdn-images-1.medium.com/max/1300/0*nqOM-zWVnd2LPoUR.png" w...

item.title: crewAIInc / crewAI
item.description: ⭐ 148 GitHub stars today · ⭐ 49.8k total stars — Framework for orchestrating role-playing, autonomous AI agents. By fostering collaborative intelligence, CrewAI empowers agents to work together seamlessly, tackling complex tasks.
